blockingIssues.description: SDK dual-module packaging is still broken for CommonJS consumers: package is `type: module` while `exports.require` points to `dist/cjs/index.js`, and `require('/Users/maxmohammadi/cash-orderbook/sdk')` fails at runtime.
Indexer applies event types in fixed order with `OrderFilled` before `TradeEvent`; full-fill maker orders can be deleted before trade reconciliation, causing stale depth and missed orderbook deltas.

appliedUpdates.description: Added `.factory/library/backend-event-ordering.md` documenting the cross-event ordering invariant for per-type Aptos polling and why OrderFilled-before-TradeEvent can corrupt depth reconciliation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When feature scope includes dual ESM/CJS packaging, add an explicit runtime compatibility check for both `import` and `require` (not just build artifact presence).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round-2 review of fix-sdk-build-api still found `require` failure despite passing test/typecheck/lint and dual-output build commands.

rejectedObservations.observation: backend-worker tests-first deviation noted in fix-sdk-build-api review
backend-worker tests-first/manual-runtime verification deviation noted in fix-indexer-ws-live review
